items.title: Iran says no new commitments on nuclear sites after Vance says inspectors to be invited back
items.summary: Iran's foreign ministry says it made "no new commitments" on nuclear inspections after talks in Switzerland.

items.title: Trump anticipates better relationship with Colombia under new leader
items.summary: Abelardo de la Espriella, who preliminary results suggest is Colombia's next president, had Trump's endorsement.

items.title: Ransom note claims Nancy Guthrie died after abduction
items.summary: The note from the possible kidnappers reportedly stated that they did not mean for her to die and included an apology to the family.

items.title: Montreal shooting leaves officer, civilian and suspect dead
items.summary: It was the first time in 24 years that a Montreal police officer was  killed in the line of duty, the city's police chief said.

items.title: Inquiry ordered after building fire kills 15 in north India city
items.summary: Witnesses described people jumping from windows to escape, while rescue teams broke through a wall to save lives.

items.title: Dettol apologises after ad to clean up 'toxic men' backfires in China
items.summary: The ad sparked accusations of sexism in China, after featuring a man looking for a partner who is "not tainted by other men".

items.title: Myanmar army killed over 700 civilians in six months, UN says
items.summary: The new UN report says the 702 civilian deaths over six months last year included 153 children.

items.title: Russian troop build-up threatens city seen as key to seizing Ukraine's Donbas
items.summary: If Kostyantynivka falls, Russian forces would be able push towards Ukraine's last remaining strongholds in the east.

items.title: Tesla crash that killed a woman under US federal investigation
items.summary: A Tesla driver on Friday said he was using ‘self-driving’ technology after his vehicle sped into a Texas home.

items.title: At least 13 killed and dozens injured after Qatar gas explosion
items.summary: The city's main liquified natural gas (LNG) processing site suffered a "technical accident" in the Ras Laffan industrial zone.

items.title: Kenya minister found in contempt of court over US-backed Ebola centre
items.summary: Aden Duale defied a High Court order to stop building work on a controversial US-funded facility.

items.title: Largest ever cocaine bust in Australia after police raid underground bunker
items.summary: Police seized 2.7 tonnes of cocaine worth an estimated A$816m after searching a property in western Sydney.

items.title: Clive Davis, music mogul behind Whitney Houston and Bruce Springsteen, dies aged 94
items.summary: The record company executive also worked with Billy Joel, Pink Floyd, Alicia Keys and Aerosmith.

items.title: Red heat alerts issued in France, Italy and Spain as 40C temperatures forecast
items.summary: The heatwave conditions are forecast to intensify in the coming days across central and western Europe.

items.title: Trump-backed political outsider wins Colombia election, initial count shows
items.summary: Abelardo de la Espriella appears to have narrowly defeated his rival Iván Cepeda, who says the preliminary count is "not yet official or binding".

items.title: Former Kenyan justice minister blocked from entering Uganda, lawyers' body says
items.summary: Martha  Karua is a lawyer representing detained Ugandan opposition leader Kizza Besigye who is on trial for treason.

items.title: Trump says repairs to algae-plagued Reflecting Pool will begin immediately
items.summary: Despite a multi-million dollar renovation, the Washington DC landmark has peeling paint and algae. Trump claims vandals are partly to blame.

items.title: How 100 hospitals switched to pen and paper to defeat a national cyber-attack
items.summary: For four days, dozens of Romanian hospitals went offline, as cyber-experts sought to defeat the hackers.

items.title: A brutal gang rape in India revives painful memories of 2012 Delhi assault
items.summary: The case from Begusarai in Bihar comes close in brutality to that inflicted on a woman during a sexual assault in 2012.

items.title: 'I witnessed Maradona's Hand of God' - a goal still talked about 40 years on
items.summary: One BBC journalist's first football game had one of the sport's most controversial moments and also one of the most celebrated.

items.title: Is Germany looking again at coal-powered electricity?
items.summary: It had planned to abandon the fuel, but the higher cost of natural gas may make it think again.

items.title: The BTS fans losing thousands as scammers cash in on comeback tour ticket war
items.summary: As ticket demand outstrips supply by a ratio of 15 to one, desperate fans have become easy targets.

items.title: Ethiopia PM's party wins landslide as fears grow of new conflict
items.summary: Abiy Ahmed's party retains its huge majority despite unrest in several parts of Ethiopia and tensions with its neighbours.

items.title: Japanese pop group XG went from brutal five year training to global pop stars
items.summary: The Japanese pop group have become global stars, after being recruited before they were teenagers.

items.title: Why was JD Vance in a luxury Swiss resort for talks with Iran?
items.summary: BBC correspondent Jessica Parker explains some key points of the direct talks between the US vice-president and Iran.

items.title: Parisians cool off in canal amid 'red alert' heatwave
items.summary: France has issued red heatwave alerts for around half the country as temperatures soar to record levels.

items.title: BBC sees destroyed villages in Israeli-occupied southern Lebanon
items.summary: Travelling with a humanitarian convoy, BBC's Hugo Bachega has been given rare access to a part of Lebanon under Israeli occupation.

items.title: Would you choose to take a 22-hour non-stop flight?
items.summary: The BBC asked Sydney locals if they would take the newly announced, longest ever commercial flight from Sydney to London.

items.title: Should the US impose a teen social media ban like the UK?
items.summary: The UK is enforcing a social media ban for children under the age of 16. The BBC asked Americans of different ages if they thought it was a good idea.

items.title: Will Putin change tactics after Ukrainian drone attacks?
items.summary: Steve Rosenberg explains what Russia's next steps may be after frequent Ukrainian drone attacks.
